name: Tomato Beef Stir-Fry
recipeIngredient: Tomato wedges (3 medium, cut into 6 wedges per tomato)
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut each tomato into 6 wedges.
2. Cut the green bell pepper into 1-inch squares.
3. Cut celery into 1/4-inch slanted slices.
4. Cut beef into 1 1/2-inch wide strips against the grain, then cut into 1/8-inch slices across the grain.
5. In a bowl, stir together cornstarch, soy sauce, sherry, and water. Add meat and stir to coat. Then add 1 1/2 teaspoons of oil and marinate for at least 15 minutes.
6. Meanwhile, prepare the cooking sauce and set aside.
7. Place a stir-fry pan over high heat until hot. Add 2 tablespoons of oil when the oil begins to heat.
8. When the oil is hot, add ginger and garlic and stir once.
9. Add the meat mixture and stir fry until the meat is browned (1½ to 2 minutes).
10. Remove from the pan and set aside.
11. Pour remaining oil into the pan. When hot, add celery and onion and stir fry for 1 minute.
12. Add bell pepper and stir fry for another minute.
13. If the pan looks dry, add a few drops of water.
14. Add tomatoes and stir fry for 1 minute.
15. Return meat to the pan and add the cooking sauce. Stir until the sauce thickens. Season with salt to taste.
16. Makes 4 servings
